info.name: The Things Stack — Application Server
ApplicationRegistry

List applications where the given user or organization is a direct collaborator.
If no user or organization is given, this returns the applications the caller
has access to.
Similar to Get, this selects the fields given by the field mask.
More or less fields may be returned, depending on the rights of the caller.

params.name: collaborator.organization_ids.organization_id
params.description: This ID shares namespace with user IDs.

params.name: collaborator.user_ids.user_id
params.description: This ID shares namespace with organization IDs.

params.name: collaborator.user_ids.email
params.description: Secondary identifier, which can only be used in specific requests.

params.name: field_mask
params.description: The names of the application fields that should be returned.

params.name: order
params.description:
Order the results by this field path (must be present in the field mask).
Default ordering is by ID. Prepend with a minus (-) to reverse the order.

params.name: limit
params.description: Limit the number of results per page.

params.name: page
params.description: Page number for pagination. 0 is interpreted as 1.

params.name: deleted
params.description: Only return recently deleted applications.

items.docs:
List applications where the given user or organization is a direct collaborator.
If no user or organization is given, this returns the applications the caller
has access to.
Similar to Get, this selects the fields given by the field mask.
More or less fields may be returned, depending on the rights of the caller.

info.name: Update the application, changing the fields specified by the field mask to the provided values.

items.docs: Update the application, changing the fields specified by the field mask to the provided values.

info.name:
Get the application with the given identifiers, selecting the fields specified
in the field mask.
More or less fields may be returned, depending on the rights of the caller.

params.name: field_mask
params.description: The names of the application fields that should be returned.

items.docs:
Get the application with the given identifiers, selecting the fields specified
in the field mask.
More or less fields may be returned, depending on the rights of the caller.

info.name:
Delete the application. This may not release the application ID for reuse.
All end devices must be deleted from the application before it can be deleted.

items.docs:
Delete the application. This may not release the application ID for reuse.
All end devices must be deleted from the application before it can be deleted.

info.name:
Request DevEUI from the configured address block for a device inside the application.
The maximum number of DevEUI's issued per application can be configured.

items.docs:
Request DevEUI from the configured address block for a device inside the application.
The maximum number of DevEUI's issued per application can be configured.

info.name:
Purge the application. This will release the application ID for reuse.
All end devices must be deleted from the application before it can be deleted.
The application owner is responsible for clearing data from any (external) integrations
that may store and expose data by application ID

items.docs:
Purge the application. This will release the application ID for reuse.
All end devices must be deleted from the application before it can be deleted.
The application owner is responsible for clearing data from any (external) integrations
that may store and expose data by application ID

info.name: Restore a recently deleted application.

items.docs:
Deployment configuration may specify if, and for how long after deletion,
entities can be restored.

info.name:
Update the rights of an API key of the application.
This method can also be used to delete the API key, by giving it no rights.
The caller is required to have all assigned or/and removed rights.

params.name: api_key.id
params.description:
Immutable and unique public identifier for the API key.
Generated by the Access Server.

info.name: AsEndDeviceBatchRegistry

Delete a list of devices within the same application.
This operation is atomic; either all devices are deleted or none.
Devices not found are skipped and no error is returned.

items.docs:
Delete a list of devices within the same application.
This operation is atomic; either all devices are deleted or none.
Devices not found are skipped and no error is returned.

info.name: AsEndDeviceRegistry

If there are multiple matches, an error will be returned.

info.name: As

Get a link configuration from the Application Server to Network Server.
This only contains the configuration. Use GetLinkStats to view statistics and any link errors.

items.docs:
Get a link configuration from the Application Server to Network Server.
This only contains the configuration. Use GetLinkStats to view statistics and any link errors.

info.name:
Set a link configuration from the Application Server a Network Server.
This call returns immediately after setting the link configuration; it does not wait for a link to establish.
To get link statistics or errors, use GetLinkStats.
Note that there can only be one Application Server instance linked to a Network Server for a given application at a time.

items.docs:
Set a link configuration from the Application Server a Network Server.
This call returns immediately after setting the link configuration; it does not wait for a link to establish.
To get link statistics or errors, use GetLinkStats.
Note that there can only be one Application Server instance linked to a Network Server for a given application at a time.

info.name: Delete the link between the Application Server and Network Server for the specified application.

items.docs: Delete the link between the Application Server and Network Server for the specified application.

info.name:
GetLinkStats returns the link statistics.
This call returns a NotFound error code if there is no link for the given application identifiers.
This call returns the error code of the link error if linking to a Network Server failed.

items.docs:
GetLinkStats returns the link statistics.
This call returns a NotFound error code if there is no link for the given application identifiers.
This call returns the error code of the link error if linking to a Network Server failed.

info.name: As_GetConfiguration
ApplicationUpStorage
Returns a stream of application messages that have been stored in the database.

params.name: type
params.description: Query upstream messages of a specific type. If not set, then all upstream messages are returned.

params.name: end_device_ids.dev_eui
params.description: The LoRaWAN DevEUI.

params.name: end_device_ids.join_eui
params.description: The LoRaWAN JoinEUI (AppEUI until LoRaWAN 1.0.3 end devices).

params.name: end_device_ids.dev_addr
params.description: The LoRaWAN DevAddr.

params.name: limit
params.description: Limit number of results.

params.name: after
params.description: Query upstream messages after this timestamp only. Cannot be used in conjunction with last.

params.name: before
params.description: Query upstream messages before this timestamp only. Cannot be used in conjunction with last.

params.name: f_port
params.description: Query uplinks on a specific FPort only.

params.name: order
params.description: Order results.

params.name: field_mask
params.description:
The names of the upstream message fields that should be returned. See the API reference
for allowed field names for each type of upstream message.

params.name: last
params.description: Query upstream messages that have arrived in the last minutes or hours. Cannot be used in conjunction with after and before.

params.name: continuation_token
params.description: The continuation token, which is used to retrieve the next page. If provided, other fields are ignored.

items.docs: Returns a stream of application messages that have been stored in the database.
